The creator strongly advises investors to have a plan before buying stocks and to understand what they own. Do not buy random tickers and then ask 'are you still holding this' in comments. Understand the technical setup, fundamental thesis, and your risk tolerance before entering positions.

Many AI and semi-conductor stocks have technical setups that are not attractive for swing trading in the near term, including Broadcom and Qualcomm, despite strong long-term investment cases. Do not treat all stocks created equal; some are swing trades (Marvell) while others are 5-10 year holds (Broadcom, Qualcomm).

High-beta semiconductor and AI stocks should be hedged with 5-10% of portfolio in out-of-the-money puts expiring in 2-3 months to protect against violent pushdowns while still allowing portfolio to benefit from rallies. This is critical risk management for AI-heavy portfolios.

Tesla appears very overvalued using traditional metrics, but the investment thesis depends entirely on execution of robotics strategy and potential SpaceX collaboration catalysts. The stock may face a slow summer absent these catalysts, then break out if announcements emerge. The thesis is speculative.

iSOJcKD5ow4.title: Top 5 AI GROWTH Stocks to BUY NOW (Huge Comebacks)

buy_recommendations.reason: Qualcomm is focusing on the CPU shortage and entering new markets including data center and AI. The creator owns this stock as an entry-level position. The stock is trading at a forward PE ratio of 17.93, which the creator believes is cheap for a semiconductor company transitioning to data center and AI plays. In their most recent investor day, Qualcomm raised their fiscal 2029 non-handset revenue target to $40 billion (approximately double the prior target) and targeted more than $18 non-GAAP earnings per share. At a forward PE of 27 (considered very low for a data center semiconductor company), this implies a stock price of $486, representing nearly a threebagger from the current price of $176. Key catalysts include custom silicon revenue beginning in second half of 2026, two hyperscaler ASIC customers entering production, meaningful data center contribution in Q1 fiscal 2027 (Oct-Dec 2026), Microsoft Azure deployment of the AI250 HBC chip, and at least $5 billion in data center revenue expected for fiscal 2027. The Meta partnership for multi-generation CPUs starting in 2028 also provides validation of demand.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: Current price approximately $176; creator is buying gradually as an entry-level position
buy_recommendations.target_price: $486 based on fiscal 2029 earnings guidance of $18 non-GAAP EPS at 27x forward PE
buy_recommendations.cautions: Execution risk is significant as this is a turnaround story. All projections are 2-3 years out and must actually materialize. Risk that compute constraints end or shortages resolve sooner than expected. Competitive risk from Nvidia and others. Notable concern: Qualcomm was removed from Nvidia's NVLink Fusion CPU partners list, which is a potential red flag for adoption. The custom silicon, HPC accelerators, and Azure partnership are key validation points but execution must follow through.

Current market cap is $2.8 billion and the stock is down roughly 31%, presenting a potential entry point. Kion has 341 MW of energized capacity and 430 MW of secure capacity with a pipeline of 2.2 gigawatts. Power availability is identified as the single bottleneck in the AI market, making this space attractive. First high-performance computing revenue is not expected until 2027, likely Q2. The company has transitioned from Bitcoin mining to HPC.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: Stock down 31% from recent highs; creator finds current valuation more attractive but notes high risk/high reward profile
buy_recommendations.cautions: This is a high-risk, high-reward investment in a very volatile market with a small player. No HPC revenue until 2027. Stock is highly volatile. Zero live HPC critical IT operational currently. Major catalyst expected in Q4 2026 with Scrub Grass detail load study results that could add 750 MW capacity, but this is not guaranteed. Creator owns other players in this space (Iran, CoreWeave) but this is more speculative.

buy_recommendations.reason: Broadcom is one of the big players in the AI space with multiple growth catalysts. Forward PE ratio of 22.89 appears reasonable given growth expectations. Full-year 2026 AI revenue is expected to be $56 billion, up 180% from the prior year. The company has multiple agreements with hyperscalers including OpenAI, Meta, and others for XPU deployments, with expectations of six total hyperscaler customers by 2027. Broadcom has reiterated guidance for over $100 billion in AI revenue for fiscal 2027. The company has strategically created an XPU platform with contracted AI solutions and partnerships (Apollo, Blackstone as leading investors) to lock in power for deploying more than 20 gigawatts of compute capacity by 2028, which addresses the primary bottleneck in AI chip sales. Management recently extended visibility beyond 2027 into 2028 for continued AI revenue growth. The optics segment also benefits from massive shortage conditions in the semiconductor supply chain.
buy_recommendations.cautions: Stock is down nearly 25% from peak but valuation is not cheap. Primary risk is execution on delivering the promised AI revenue growth and maintaining partnerships with hyperscalers. Competition from custom silicon solutions from major cloud providers is a threat. The extension of revenue visibility to 2028 is positive but 2028 and beyond involve more uncertainty.

buy_recommendations.reason: This is a major player in the optics space that has experienced significant growth (up 700% at peak) but is down nearly a third from all-time highs. The creator noted the optics space is becoming more attractive following the drawdown. The company reports insane supply shortages across multiple areas: EML, laser pumps (the biggest unexpected shortage), OCS (optical circuit switch), and CPO (co-packaged optics) applications. The CEO expects to increase supply line by 50% over the next year (December quarter to December quarter), but the company continues to report supply-demand imbalance greater than 30% and is significantly under-shipping demand, having to make choices on which customers to support. This supply shortage environment can dramatically increase margins and earnings per share, similar to what occurred in the memory space. Multiple growth catalysts expected: CPO scale out revenue ramp and additional OCS in Q4 2026; Nvidia multi-generational CPO laser purchase order delivery in H2 2027; optical scale-up shipping begins in calendar 2028; optical scale-up on market plus new fab online in 2028.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: Stock down from all-time highs; creator finding this more attractive after drawdown but not yet committing
buy_recommendations.cautions: Forward PE ratio is 46, which is elevated and the biggest valuation risk. The bulls argue that 46x is not excessive given expected revenue and earnings growth, and that the forward PE will contract rapidly as growth continues. However, valuation remains a significant concern. Supply-demand imbalance dynamics could shift. Creator owns other optics plays but is 'starting to get more into' this space, indicating still evaluating.

buy_recommendations.reason: Micron is trading at a forward PE ratio of 6.8, which the creator describes as 'insane' valuation. The recent earnings showed that tight memory conditions are expected to persist beyond calendar year 2027, driving strategic customer agreements. These agreements are expected to provide recurring revenue and recurring margins, potentially transforming Micron from a commodity memory supplier into a partnership-based business warranting a higher valuation multiple. The HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) space presents significant growth opportunities with Micron as one of only three capable players globally (alongside SK Hynix and Samsung). Moving from HBM3/3E to HBM4, HBM4E, HBM5, and custom HBM solutions requires sustained innovation and strong partnerships, creating barriers to entry. Supply-demand shortage will persist even with increased supply in 2028 because demand growth continues to exceed supply growth. New Chinese competitors entering the HBM space will not quickly threaten established players due to the rate of innovation and partnership requirements.
buy_recommendations.cautions: Memory space is fundamentally a commodity business with risk of sudden supply increases from competitors. The creator did not own Micron (stated regret about this) and notes that even with the bullish thesis, the stock could be trading at single-digit forward PE for fundamental reasons. Supply could increase faster than anticipated or demand could falter. The creator emphasizes this stock represents a middle ground between bullish and bearish cases. Not being in this position, the creator has chosen to focus capital on other semiconductor opportunities.

key_warnings: AI and semiconductor markets are currently crashing with numerous shortages across optics, CPUs, memory, and power infrastructure. While the creator is bullish long-term, the market is experiencing a down phase and many of these stocks are down significantly from peaks (20-30% pullbacks in some cases).

Power availability is identified as the single greatest bottleneck in the AI market. Any company betting on AI growth must have access to sufficient power, and this constraint will persist through 2028+. Companies without secured power capacity face execution risk.

The two biggest risks across all recommendations are (1) execution risk - management must deliver on ambitious guidance that is often 2-3 years out, and (2) valuation risk - some of these stocks still trade at elevated multiples (LRCX at 46x forward PE, AVGO at 22.89x) and depend on growth assumptions materializing.

Nvidia remains the dominant player in AI chips and custom silicon solutions from hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) pose competitive threats to traditional chip designers like Qualcomm and Broadcom.

The supply-demand dynamics that are creating shortages today could reverse if supply comes online faster than expected or if demand growth slows. This particularly applies to memory (MU) and optics (LRCX) where the creator acknowledges commodity market risks.

rWXXooBZXIY.title: Top Stocks I'm Buying For Massive Growth In July 2026

buy_recommendations.reason: Coreweave is a necloud company that rents GPU compute capacity to AI companies. Despite a 10-15% decline following Meta's announcement of their own cloud business, the creator believes Coreweave will continue operating at full capacity because Meta cannot resell leased capacity and only plans to rent excess capacity they own. Meta has $35 billion committed to Coreweave as a customer and is not building excess capacity, meaning demand for AI infrastructure from neocloud providers will remain strong. The creator is buying this stock especially as prices continue to fall.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines
buy_recommendations.cautions: Neocloud companies have high upfront costs for data centers, GPUs, and grid-connected power. As small, unprofitable companies, they must raise capital through dilution or high-interest loans, which extends their path to profitability. Meta Platforms' competitive entry into the cloud market poses a long-term risk, though near-term capacity constraints should protect current demand.

buy_recommendations.reason: Nebius is a necloud provider renting GPU compute capacity, down 10-15% following Meta's cloud announcement. The creator is still buying because Meta's $27 billion commitment to Nebius is structured so that Meta can only access $15 billion of optional capacity if Nebius cannot fill those racks with other customers first. This means Nebius has locked in significant revenue and will continue to operate at full capacity given the explosive demand for AI infrastructure. High utilization rates and capacity constraints ensure neocloud companies will remain profitable in the near term.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines
buy_recommendations.cautions: Nebius relies on the structured deal with Meta and must fill remaining capacity with other customers to maximize profitability. As a smaller, unprofitable necloud company, it faces higher capital raising costs and interest rate pressures. The long-term competitive threat from Meta Platforms' own cloud business could pressure margins over time.

buy_recommendations.reason: Iron is a necloud company that rents GPU capacity, down significantly (10-15%+) after Meta's cloud announcement. Despite this decline, the creator is buying because companies renting GPUs will continue operating at full capacity given insatiable demand for AI infrastructure. Meta's $35 billion commitment to Coreweave and similar customer commitments to other neocloud providers mean these companies will remain fully utilized even as Meta eventually enters the market. The creator expects neocloud companies to keep generating strong returns from capacity constraints.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines
buy_recommendations.cautions: Neocloud companies face significant capital intensity, requiring massive upfront investment in data centers and GPUs. As unprofitable or less profitable companies, they must raise capital at unfavorable terms in a high-interest-rate environment. Meta Platforms' eventual market entry poses a structural competitive risk to long-term margins and market share.

buy_recommendations.reason: Nvidia holds over 90% share of the data center GPU market. Despite being down over 10% from their latest earnings, the creator believes this is an attractive entry point because every earnings call shows demand is accelerating, not slowing. All major AI companies—including OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Google, and others—are buying every Nvidia GPU they can access, limited only by supply constraints. Nvidia is supply-constrained and remains essential infrastructure for the AI buildout, making the stock weakness an opportunity.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 10%+
buy_recommendations.cautions: Market sentiment has turned fearful, causing 10-20-30% declines in AI stocks despite strong underlying demand. The stock declined on concerns that hyperscalers may not see returns on their record AI infrastructure spending, though this appears to be temporary market weakness rather than fundamental deterioration.

buy_recommendations.reason: Broadcom designs all custom AI chips (ASICs) for Google, Meta, Anthropic, OpenAI, and at least two other major AI companies. The creator notes that Broadcom designed the OpenAI Jalapeno chip in just 9 months (versus the typical 2-3 year timeline), demonstrating exceptional design velocity and competitive positioning. Despite being down over 20% from their latest earnings, the creator believes this weakness is unwarranted because demand for custom chips is accelerating, not slowing. Every major AI company is locked in with Broadcom for custom chip design, providing a stable revenue stream.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 20%+
buy_recommendations.cautions: Market sentiment has turned fearful on concerns about hyperscalers' returns on AI infrastructure spending. Broadcom's earnings guidance disappointed the market, triggering a 10% single-day decline. However, the creator views this as temporary market weakness rather than a fundamental problem.

buy_recommendations.reason: Lumenum makes the lasers and optical transceivers that convert electrical signals from chips into light for fiber optic cables in data center interconnects. Optical networking is becoming a major bottleneck for AI performance, as thousands of chips must pass massive amounts of data between each other. Nvidia invested $2 billion directly into Lumenum to lock in capacity and accelerate their own networking R&D. Despite this major vote of confidence and 2+ billion in locked-in revenue, the stock is still down 10-15%, making it cheaper without getting weaker.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 10-15%
buy_recommendations.cautions: Optical networking stocks are capital-intensive and dependent on continued hyperscaler spending. Market has temporarily lost confidence in all AI-related infrastructure despite locked-in multibillion-dollar commitments from Nvidia, Meta, and Amazon.

buy_recommendations.reason: Coherent makes a broad range of optical components—transceivers, lasers, and amplifiers—for data center interconnects and long-haul telecommunications networks. Optical interconnects are critical bottleneck technology for modern AI data centers. Nvidia invested $2 billion directly into Coherent to lock in capacity and accelerate R&D. This major strategic investment from the leading AI infrastructure company validates the criticality of Coherent's technology, yet the stock is still down 10-20%, providing an attractive entry point.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 10-20%
buy_recommendations.cautions: Optical networking companies are dependent on continued hyperscaler capex spending. Market sentiment has turned negative on all AI infrastructure despite multibillion-dollar locked-in revenue commitments from Nvidia, Meta, and Amazon.

buy_recommendations.reason: Corning makes glass fiber optic cables—essential infrastructure for modern AI data centers. They have lined up a $6 billion deal with Meta Platforms, a $3.2 billion deal with Nvidia, and a multi-billion-dollar supply agreement with Amazon. These are not just purchase orders but represent years of locked-in revenue and demonstrable confidence from the world's largest AI companies. Despite these massive, cash-backed commitments, Corning's stock is down 10-20%, making it cheaper without getting fundamentally weaker.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 10-20%
buy_recommendations.cautions: Corning is dependent on sustained hyperscaler capex spending. The optical fiber market is experiencing temporary weakness despite multibillion-dollar locked-in supply agreements.

buy_recommendations.reason: Qualcomm recently announced its Dragonfly C1000 CPU (with 250+ cores) specifically designed for large-scale AI workloads in data centers. At their investor day, Qualcomm nearly doubled their revenue target outside smartphones to $40 billion by fiscal 2029, with $15 billion specifically aimed at AI data centers via the C1000. Meta Platforms and Microsoft Azure have both signed multi-generation supply agreements for the C1000. The creator notes the stock is down ~15% despite landing two of the most important customers in AI computing and doubling their data center revenue target. This represents the market pricing Qualcomm's entire data center business at zero, which seems unwarranted given locked-in major customer commitments.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: during current market weakness and price declines of 15%
buy_recommendations.cautions: The Dragonfly C1000 does not go into mass production for another 2 years, making this an early-stage investment. Qualcomm will compete with AMD's EPYC CPUs and Intel's Xeon chips in the data center CPU market. However, the creator notes Qualcomm has experience competing with AMD and Intel in other markets.

buy_recommendations.reason: SK Hynix is expected to begin trading on NASDAQ on July 10th as an ADR (American Depository Receipt), giving US investors direct access to the dominant leader in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for the first time. HBM is critical specialized stacked memory that sits next to every AI chip and feeds it data fast enough to prevent idle time. SK Hynix holds a dominant 60% share of the HBM market. The entire HBM market is sold out well into 2027, with SK Hynix's chairman warning the memory shortage could extend to 2030. The company is tripling revenues year-over-year with over 70% operating margins—software-like profitability from a 43-year-old hardware company. The IPO represents a generational opportunity to own the leader of the memory super cycle.
buy_recommendations.when_to_buy: IPO trading begins July 10th on NASDAQ
buy_recommendations.cautions: HBM is extremely capital-intensive, taking 4-5 years to build specialized fabs. Production requires 3x more factory space than normal memory with lower yields. However, the extreme scarcity extending to 2027-2030 and SK Hynix's dominant 60% market share should sustain pricing power and profitability. IPO timing and pricing unknown; subject to market volatility.

key_warnings: Market sentiment has swung from greed (CNN Fear and Greed Index of 71 in early May) to extreme fear (index of 24 last week) on concerns that hyperscalers (Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Meta) are making record AI infrastructure commitments without clear near-term returns. This has caused broad-based declines of 10-30% in quality AI infrastructure stocks despite strong underlying demand and locked-in multibillion-dollar revenue commitments.

Interest rates remain above 3% and the Federal Reserve has not lowered rates. Higher interest rates increase the cost of borrowing for growth companies, extending their path to profitability and reducing their present-day valuations. This particularly impacts neocloud companies that must raise capital to fund data center construction.

Optical networking and AI data center interconnects are emerging as a major bottleneck for AI performance. Demand for optical transceivers, lasers, and fiber optic cables is outpacing supply with lead times stretching from months to a full year. This creates both opportunity (for suppliers) and risk (if capacity constraints are not addressed).

The Korean market's 25% crash and 900,000+ liquidations mirror the excessive leverage and speculation of the US dot-com bubble. This is a warning sign of systemic leverage in global markets. While a bottom may be forming in Korea, the cascading liquidations suggest potential contagion risks if liquidations spread to other markets.

Do NOT fall into 'Target shopper mentality' where you buy stocks just because they are down big or short them just because they hit an all-time high. IBM is down 22%—but the creator warned that 'just because something is down a lot doesn't mean it's a good buy.' Similarly, Apple hit an all-time high—but shorting it is equally misguided. Always analyze WHY a stock is moving before entering a position. Overshooting (both up and down) is common, but impulsive reaction trades based on price alone lead to losses.

Forward guidance and expected future earnings matter more than one-quarter miss. IBM's miss revealed a structural shift in capex allocation, not just a quarter of underperformance. Always dig into the reason for earnings misses or outperformance; surface-level price reactions can lead to poor decisions.

The Fed's new Chair Worsh emphasizes independence and price stability as the top priority. He has appointed task forces to review Fed communications, balance sheet policy, inflation frameworks, and other areas. The Fed is NOT providing forward guidance on interest rates anymore and may reduce forward guidance overall. Do not expect the Fed to telegraph rate cuts in advance; stay flexible.

Meta is at a critical year-long trend line but has broken out multiple times and failed. Do NOT buy prematurely into a breakout that lacks confirmation. Wait for a clear break AND retest before entering. 'Buying Meta prematurely here is just a guess' and 'not a great trade' without confirmation.

However, some software stocks like Microsoft and Palantir are showing relative strength and recovery. Focus on stocks showing relative strength WITHIN the weakness, not the entire sector.
